notes: Integrated from the real Grade 2 Quarter 1, Book 4 source file.
Early card: shorter text, short sentences, easiest independent start.

book.title: Stone Soup
book.description: Stone Soup includes a six-chapter reading sequence, vocabulary, comprehension questions, journal/writing prompts, and assessment checks.

choiceGuidance.reason: shorter text, short sentences, easiest independent start

weeks.title: Stone Soup Book Card
vocabulary: 1. traveler - a person who goes from place to place
2. village - a small town with a few houses
1. spare - to give something you don't really need
2. pretend - to act like something that isn't true
1. announced - said something in a loud, clear voice
2. curious - wanting to know more about something
1. boiling - water that is very hot and bubbling
2. bubbling - making bubbles as it cooks
2. community - a group of people who live in the same place
1. crowd - a large group of people
2. sharing - giving some of what you have to others
journal: Write about how one small act of sharing can help a whole group.
Explain how the villagers change during the story. Use evidence from the beginning and end.
writing: Write about how one small act of sharing can help a whole group.
Explain how the villagers change during the story. Use evidence from the beginning and end.
questions: 2. What did the woman do when the traveler asked for food?
1. What did the traveler notice about the village?
2. Why wouldn't the villagers share their food?
1. What did the traveler say his stone could do?
2. How did the villagers feel about the stone soup?
1. What was the first ingredient the traveler asked for?
2. How did the villagers respond to the traveler's requests?
1. What brought more villagers to the town square?
2. How were the villagers feeling as they waited?
1. What did the traveler do with his stone when the soup was ready?
2. What was the real magic in the story?
assessment: Use chapter comprehension questions for daily checks.
Use journal/writing prompt as written-response evidence.
